title: poa pannonica subsp. scabra
intro: Poa pannonica subsp. scabra, also known as Pannonian meadow-grass, is a subspecies of perennial grass native to Europe, particularly in the Pannonian region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's health benefits include soothing irritated throats, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Poa pannonica subsp. scabra can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
